batch_summary: Deed [315] — Heirship Affidavit for W. E. Black and Ida A. Black (LC Pg. 306, Doc# 3774, executed 1965-06-08, filed 1965-07-26, recorded 1965-07-28). Companion instrument to deed [314] (affidavit of title, LC Pg. 304-305, same affiants, same date, same filing session). Affiants W. D. Bounds and Jeff Bounds establish the complete list of W. E. Black and Ida A. Black's children: (1) Hallie Black m. J. R. Milburn, (2) Ella Black m. George Rowell/G. L. Rowell, (3) Minnie Black m. W. D. Swint, (4) Zona Black m. P. Thompson/F. P. Thompson, (5) C. E. Black (wife Erna), (6) J. A. Black (wife Leta; in Arizona, separated from wife, Jan 2, 1943), (7) J. T. Black/John T. Black (wife Verona E. Black). No adoptions. Ida A. Black died intestate before 1943; no administration; estate < $10,000. W. E. Black had not remarried as of January 2, 1943 — the date of the ISSUE-43 item 4 deed (C.E. Black + → JT Black). All children over 21 by that date. This upgrades JT Black's parentage from 'probable' to 'confirmed' and identifies all 7 potential grantors in the 1943 conveyance. C. E. Black reclassified from 'probable sibling' to confirmed brother of JT Black.

01_FAMILY_ENTITY_STRUCTURE.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [315] — W.E. Black + Ida A. Black heirship affidavit: parentage confirmed (was probable); complete family tree (7 children identified, was 2); C.E. Black reclassified from probable sibling to confirmed brother

operations.description: Replace W.E. Black family tree block — upgrade from 'probable' to 'confirmed' parentage, add all 7 children with spouses, add Ida A. Black death info

W. E. Black  +  Ida A. Black

     ├── John Thomas "JT" Black (10/21/1887–1/30/1959)  ← see below
     ├── C. E. Black (probable sibling; first-named grantor in 1943 deed, ISSUE-43 item 4)
     └── [other children unknown]

JT Black's parents (confirmed — per deeds [314] + [315], 1965):

     │              (W.E. Black had not remarried as of 1/2/1943; 7 children, no adoptions)
     ├── Hallie Black — m. J. R. Milburn
     ├── Ella Black — m. George Rowell (aka G. L. Rowell)
     ├── Minnie Black — m. W. D. Swint
     ├── Zona Black — m. P. Thompson (aka F. P. Thompson)

     └── John Thomas "JT" Black (10/21/1887–1/30/1959; wife Verona E. Black)  ← see below

03_DEED_CHAIN_SUMMARY.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [315] added to Phase 2 — Heirship Affidavit, W.D. Bounds + Jeff Bounds re: W.E. Black and Ida A. Black family (1965); companion to deed [314]

operations.description: Add deed [315] entry to Phase 2, after deed [314]
operations.content: 5. `1965-06-08_[315]_l` — W. D. Bounds + Jeff Bounds (affiants) → Public (heirship affidavit, Limestone) — re: W. E. Black and Ida A. Black family. Affiants (each over 21, personally acquainted with W.E. Black and Ida A. Black during their lifetimes) attest: W.E. Black and Ida A. Black married once only, to each other; 7 children and no others: (1) Hallie Black m. J. R. Milburn, (2) Ella Black m. George Rowell/G. L. Rowell, (3) Minnie Black m. W. D. Swint, (4) Zona Black m. P. Thompson/F. P. Thompson, (5) C. E. Black (wife Erna), (6) J. A. Black (wife Leta; living in Arizona, separated from wife, Jan 2, 1943), (7) J. T. Black/John T. Black (wife Verona E. Black). No adoptions. Ida A. Black died intestate before 1943; no administration; estate < $10,000. W.E. Black had not remarried as of January 2, 1943. All children over 21 by January 2, 1943. **Companion instrument to deed [314]** (affidavit of title, same affiants, same date, same filing session, adjacent pages). Both instruments prepared to support 1965 Verona E. Black → Wilson sale of 56.8 ac Norvell Survey. Notary: E. B. Trotter, Limestone County. Sworn Jun 8, 1965; subscribed/acknowledged Jun 9, 1965. LC Pg. 306–307, Doc# 3774. Filed Jul 26, 1965 at 10:00 AM; recorded Jul 28, 1965 at 4:00 PM; John Kidd, County Clerk; Eleanor Popejoy, Deputy. **Key significance**: (a) Upgrades JT Black parentage from probable to confirmed; (b) Identifies all potential grantors in the 1943 C.E. Black + → JT Black deed (ISSUE-43 item 4); (c) J. A. Black in Arizona and separated from wife in 1943 — verify he joined in 1943 deed when pulled, or flag outstanding interest.

operations.description: Add deed [315] analysis note to ISSUE-01, after the [314] analysis paragraph
operations.content: - **Deed [315] analysis (2026-03-31)**: Heirship Affidavit (LC Pg. 306–307, Doc# 3774, executed 6/8/1965, filed 7/26/1965, recorded 7/28/1965). Companion instrument to deed [314], same affiants (W.D. Bounds and Jeff Bounds), same execution date, adjacent pages in county records. Affiants establish: W.E. Black and Ida A. Black married once only (to each other); 7 children and no others: (1) Hallie Black m. J. R. Milburn, (2) Ella Black m. George Rowell (aka G. L. Rowell), (3) Minnie Black m. W. D. Swint, (4) Zona Black m. P. Thompson (aka F. P. Thompson), (5) C. E. Black (wife Erna), (6) J. A. Black (wife Leta; living in Arizona, separated from wife, 1/2/1943), (7) J. T. Black (wife Verona E. Black). No adoptions. Ida A. Black died intestate before 1943, no administration, estate < $10,000. W.E. Black had not remarried as of 1/2/1943. All children over 21 by 1/2/1943. **Key findings**: (a) JT Black parentage upgraded from probable (per [314] alone) to confirmed — he is explicitly listed as child #7; (b) C.E. Black confirmed as brother, not just "probable sibling"; (c) 5 additional siblings identified for first time — these are the potential grantors behind the "+" in ISSUE-43 item 4 (C.E. Black + → JT Black, 1/2/1943); (d) J.A. Black was in Arizona and separated from wife Leta on the exact date of the item 4 deed — when that deed is pulled, verify J.A. Black joined or flag potential outstanding interest; (e) the January 2, 1943 date is established as legally significant in this affidavit (all status facts anchored to it), confirming it is the date of the W.E. Black family → JT Black conveyance. ISSUE-01 remains OPEN — resolution still depends on the Dorsett/Ennis → Simmons deed (ISSUE-43 item 11).
